title: Long-Term Care Subsidies
text:
Long-Term Care Subsidies - SupportGoWhere
Long-Term Care Subsidies
Share link
Provides means-tested subsidies for long-term care services by MOH-funded long-term care providers.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
For non-residential long-term care services by MOH-funded providers: Means-tested subsidies of up to 80%
For residential long-term care services by MOH-funded providers: Means-tested subsidies of up to 75%
From July 2026, additional subsidies will be provided for Singapore Citizens born in or before 1969
Collapse all sections
About this support

For non-residential long-term care services (e.g. Day Care, Community Rehabilitation services, Home Nursing, Home Medical)

Means-tested subsidies of up to 80%

From July 2026, additional 15% subsidies for Singapore Citizens born in and before 1969

 

 For residential long-term care services (e.g. Nursing Home)

Means-tested subsidies of up to 75%

From July 2026, additional 5% subsidies for Singapore Citizens born in and before 1969

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizen and Permanent Resident

Monthly household income per capita of less than or equal to $3,600* (or property annual value of $21,000 or below for households with no income)

Receiving long-term care services by MOH-funded providers

 

* From July 2026, individuals with monthly household income per capita of $3,601 to $4,800 will also qualify for subsidies.

How to apply?

Persons who require long-term care services can approach their doctor at the hospital, polyclinic, or Agency for Integrated Care (AIC) for assistance in applying for admission to a subsidised long-term care service.

Where can I find help?

Non-residential long-term care services

Residential long-term care services

AIC Hotline: 1800 650 6060

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit your enquiry

Find nearest AIC Link

Scheme last updated 30 Sep 2025
